NOT on the dual-write list; becomes a Supabase read in Phase 2+. name: SyncView Kasper — Queue (batch)

parameters.jsCode:
const meta = $input.first().json || {};
return [{ json: { ok: true, clients: {}, missing: meta.missing || [] } }];

parameters.content:
## SyncView Kasper — Queue (batch)

POST /webhook/kasper-queue with body `{ "slugs": ["bayavoce", …] }`.

Reads every requested `Calendar_<slug>` tab from the SyncView Calendar Sheet in TWO Google API calls (tab list + values:batchGet) instead of one calendar-get call per client, and returns:

`{ ok: true, clients: { "<slug>": { posts: […] } }, missing: ["<slug>", …] }`

- `posts` per client matches calendar-get exactly: header-keyed rows with a non-empty `id`, sorted by `order_index`, UNFORMATTED_VALUE / FORMATTED_STRING rendering.
- `missing` lists requested slugs that have no Calendar tab (batchGet would 400 on them, so they are excluded up front).

Powers the Kasper review queue in index.html (which falls back to per-client calendar-get if this endpoint fails).
